slides.title: Meet your AI presenter
slides.notes: Hi everyone! I'm an A.I. presenter, and I'm going to walk you through this deck myself. I joined this call, I'm putting the slides up on screen myself, and I'm narrating each one in my own voice. Let's dive in.

slides.title: What's happening here?
bullets: An AI agent joined your meeting
It narrates each slide out loud
It advances on its own — hands free
slides.notes: So what's going on? I'm an agent that joined your video call. The slides you're looking at — I'm showing them myself. I'm speaking the narration for each one, and when I finish talking, I move to the next slide by myself. Nobody's holding a clicker.

slides.title: Powered by AgentCall
bullets: One API to put an agent in any call
Works on Meet, Zoom, and Teams
slides.notes: All of this runs on AgentCall, a single A.P.I. that drops an A.I. agent into a live meeting with voice, an on-camera presence, and real-time transcription. The same way across Google Meet, Zoom, and Microsoft Teams.

slides.title: Make it your own
bullets: Write a deck as a simple JSON file
Each slide: a title, bullets, and what to say
Point it at a meeting link — and go
slides.notes: Making your own presentation is simple. You write a deck as a small JSON file. For each slide, you give a title, a few bullet points, and the words you want me to say. Then you point me at a meeting link, and I deliver the whole thing.

slides.title: Thanks for watching!
slides.notes: And that's how an A.I. agent can deliver a whole presentation, on its own, right inside your meeting. Thanks for watching, and go build your own.
